findings.description: Azure Blob Storage container 'phi-documents' in storage account 'stphiprod001' has public access level set to 'Blob'. Patient health information (PHI) including diagnostic reports and prescriptions are directly accessible without authentication via predictable blob URLs.
findings.remediation: Disable public blob access at the storage account level (AllowBlobPublicAccess=false). Use Azure AD-based access with Managed Identity for internal services, and generate time-limited SAS tokens for patient portal downloads.

findings.description: Azure SQL Server hosting the patient records database does not have Microsoft Defender for SQL enabled. SQL injection attacks, unusual access patterns, and brute-force attempts against the FHIR data store go undetected.
findings.remediation: Enable Microsoft Defender for SQL on the Azure SQL server. Configure email alerts to security team. Review current query patterns for existing anomalies.

findings.description: Azure SQL Server auditing is not enabled. HIPAA requires audit controls (§164.312(b)) — all access to PHI in the patient database must be logged including SELECT queries retrieving patient records.
findings.remediation: Enable SQL Auditing and configure logs to storage account or Log Analytics workspace with 90+ day retention. Enable diagnostic settings to capture all SQL queries.

findings.description: Azure Key Vault 'kv-healthcare-prod' does not have soft delete enabled. Accidental or malicious deletion of PHI encryption keys would make all encrypted patient records permanently inaccessible — a HIPAA availability violation.
findings.remediation: Enable soft delete and purge protection on Key Vault. Set retention days to 90. Implement Key Vault backup procedures for HIPAA disaster recovery compliance.

findings.description: AKS cluster running the FHIR API and HL7 processor containers does not have Azure Policy add-on enabled. Containers can run as root, mount host paths, or use privileged mode — all of which allow PHI extraction from co-located pods.
findings.remediation: Enable Azure Policy add-on. Assign 'Kubernetes cluster pod security restricted standards' initiative. Deploy Gatekeeper OPA policies to enforce: no root containers, no host network, no privileged containers.

findings.description: Network Security Group 'nsg-fhir-server' has an inbound rule allowing ANY source to port 443 without IP restriction. The FHIR server should only be reachable from Azure API Management and the patient portal subnet, not the public internet directly.
findings.remediation: Remove the 0.0.0.0/0 inbound rule. Add specific rules allowing only: Azure API Management service tag, patient portal subnet (10.1.2.0/24), and HL7 partner IP ranges. Use Azure Private Link for internal FHIR access.

findings.description: Azure Cosmos DB account storing unstructured clinical notes allows local authentication (key-based access) in addition to Azure AD. The primary key provides unrestricted access to all clinical note documents. Key rotation is manual and not enforced.
findings.remediation: Disable local authentication (set disableLocalAuth=true). Enforce Azure AD-only access with Managed Identity. Rotate existing keys immediately and audit all recent key-based access in diagnostic logs.

findings.description: Storage account 'stphiprod001' accepts connections using TLS 1.0 and 1.1 in addition to TLS 1.2. HIPAA transmission security (§164.312(e)(2)(ii)) requires current encryption standards. TLS 1.0/1.1 have known weaknesses.
findings.remediation: Set minimumTlsVersion to TLS1_2 on the storage account. Update all client applications that connect to this storage account to use TLS 1.2+.

findings.description: AKS cluster has Kubernetes RBAC enabled. Azure AD integration with Kubernetes RBAC is configured. ClusterRoleBindings are audited monthly.

Key Vault 'kv-healthcare-prod' does not have diagnostic logging configured. All access to PHI encryption keys (who accessed, when, what key, success/failure) must be logged under HIPAA audit controls. Currently there is no audit trail for key usage.
findings.remediation: Configure Key Vault diagnostic settings to send AuditEvent logs to Log Analytics workspace with 365-day retention. Set up Azure Monitor alerts for unauthorized key access attempts.
